synthesis_text: Individuals with symptoms of COVID-19 should consider getting tested to confirm infection. Testing is essential for those who believe they may be infected with COVID-19. Health care providers can perform accurate PCR tests for COVID-19 diagnosis. A positive antigen test indicates an active COVID-19 infection. Those diagnosed with COVID-19 should stay home and seek medical care if necessary. Test for COVID-19 if you have symptoms of the illness. If you have symptoms of COVID-19, you should get tested to confirm the infection.

You should consider getting tested for COVID-19 if you have symptoms of the illness, or if you have come in contact with the virus, testing five days after exposure is recommended. Testing is essential for those who believe they may be infected with COVID-19 to confirm the infection [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00023].

If you have symptoms of COVID-19, you should get tested to confirm the infection. Symptoms may appear 2 to 14 days after exposure, and testing is required for confirmation. If fully vaccinated and showing symptoms, contact your doctor about getting tested. Testing is essential for those who believe they may be infected with COVID-19. Children with symptoms should seek medical care and consider testing.
